title: dolicholus texanus
intro: Dolicholus texanus, also known as Texas dodder, is a parasitic flowering plant that belongs to the family Cuscutaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its antipyretic, expectorant, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Dolicholus texanus include its ability to reduce fever, relieve cough, and soothe digestive problems, making it a valuable remedy for various conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, although its use and efficacy may vary depending on the specific preparation and individual response.
